## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.292, PR #314

**Project-governance separation specification; closes R2 by principle.** Spec-only PR: defines the corpus-versus-project governance boundary and queues the migration. No files move.

### Added

- [`governance/specification-project-governance-separation.md`](../../governance/specification-project-governance-separation.md) (v1.0.1): a Specification with nine sections. §3 the classifying criterion (reusable pattern → corpus governance; filled-in operational instance of running this project → project governance), with the citation-verification family worked as the boundary case (the spec and template are reusable patterns and stay corpus; the verifications register and batch worklists are operational instances and move). §4 the one-way dependency rule (project governance may cite corpus; corpus must not cite project governance), the basis the maintainer's "not exempt" decision made cleaner than deletability; consequences: the corpus document index becomes corpus-only, and migration is citation surgery not a file move. §5 the full classification of all 40 current `governance/` artefacts (§5.1 corpus, §5.2 Phase-1 movers, §5.3 deferred). §6 the `.project-governance/` destination (dot-prefixed, its own README index, NOT added to `DEFAULT_EXEMPT_DIRS` so fully audited). §7 gate/generator consequences (corpus index corpus-only; generators exclude the dir; the two path-targeted linters re-point; a directional-dependency gate is queued). §8 the phased migration with the per-move citation-surgery discipline. §8.3 records R1 (correct in `tools/`) and closes R2 (→ Phase 1).

### Changed

- [`governance/README.md`](../../governance/README.md) (`1.10.5` to `1.10.6`): added the Specification to the Active documents table.
- [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.35` to `1.27.36`): indexed the new Specification (it is corpus governance). Note: §4 of the new spec makes this index corpus-only going forward; no project artefacts are present to remove in this PR (none have moved yet).
- [`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md): regenerated for the new corpus document via `build-taxonomy.py` and `build-portal.py`.
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): removed the R2 deferred-decision item (superseded by the spec); added three forward items (Phase-1 migration, the queued directional-dependency gate, the deferred §5.3 classifications).
- [`.working/DONE.md`](../DONE.md): R2 closure entry.

### Classification decisions (maintainer, 2026-06-24)

- `framework-document-architecture-and-interrelationship.md` stays corpus: a forking org reuses it as a valid reference document.
- `register-canonical-citations.md` stays corpus: the canonical citation list is the reusable reference; `register-citation-verifications.md` (the record of having verified those citations) is the operational instance that moves. No good justification to move the reference was found, and the criterion argues for keeping it corpus.

### Verification

- `tools/run_all_audits.sh`: expected 48/48 on the committed state (the new corpus doc is wired into README + index + generators, so no index-coverage or drift failure).
- `tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh`: expected green (D1 CHANGELOG-on-PR both files, D2 version bump, D3 CHANGELOG-dash, gate 45 TODO staleness) against the merge base.
- No em/en-dashes in the new spec; `lint-language.py` clean; house spelling (`organisation`) matches the corpus.

### Discipline observation

This PR is the decision record that retires R2 by principle rather than by fiat: instead of choosing a destination for one cluster, it defines the criterion that classifies every artefact, so future artefacts are placed correctly at creation time and the corpus does not re-accumulate operational state. The spec was drafted and held uncommitted (WIP checkpoint `796fd8e`) for maintainer plan-validation before the wiring and bookkeeping were executed, per the explicit "validate before executing" gate.

The spec ships at v1.0.1, not v1.0.0, because of a version-bump-recency catch the first CI run surfaced: the WIP split set the Version line in the `796fd8e` checkpoint but finalized the body (a one-word reword) in the wiring commit, so the history-aware gate 40 (exercised by `run-linter-regression.py`) saw "body changed since the Version was last bumped". The local pre-push checks missed it because `run_all_audits.sh` reads the working tree, not commit-by-commit history, and `run-linter-regression.py` (the history-aware runner CLAUDE.md's version-bump discipline names) was not run pre-push. The fix bumped the Version to v1.0.1 in a version-only commit so the version-line commit post-dates the body commit. Lesson reinforced: run `run-linter-regression.py` (or `lint-version-bump-recency.py`) before any push that spans more than one commit on a versioned document, not only `run_all_audits.sh`.

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.290, PR #312

**Sweep 38 close-out: two in-window residuals from the #306-#310 arc, surfaced by the `/resume` loop-break corpus-wide `/validate`.** Sweep 38 is the standing compensating control for the session-closing handoff PR #311 (which skipped its trailing `/validate-pr` + `/retro`). Full three-subagent dispatch (A, B, C) over the whole corpus plus the #306-#311 deltas; mechanical baseline 48/48; clone unshallowed before history-aware gates.

### Fixed

- **F1 (warning, C3 multi-surface incompleteness)**: [`ai/register-ai-risk.md`](../../ai/register-ai-risk.md) (`1.0.3` to `1.0.4`, Date to 2026-06-24) register-schema "Inherent Impact" field read `1 (Negligible) to 5 (Critical)`. This is the impact-5-versus-rating "Critical" conflation PR #310 set out to resolve across the risk-scoring corpus. #310 harmonized the four named risk-scoring documents to the canonical ERM scale (impact-5 = "Catastrophic"; "Critical" reserved for the 17-25 rating band) but missed this sibling AI register. The register cites the canonical methodology ([`risk/procedure-risk-assessment-methodology.md`](../../risk/procedure-risk-assessment-methodology.md) in its Related Documents) and already matched canon on the adjacent surfaces (line 37 likelihood `1 (Very Low) to 5 (Very High)`; line 43 rating `Low / Medium / High / Critical`), so its impact-5 "Critical" was a genuine residual, not an intentional AI-specific scale. Corrected to `1 (Negligible) to 5 (Catastrophic)`; the rating-top "Critical" at line 43 and the "High and Critical risks" rating-band reference at line 86 are preserved. A corpus-wide grep confirmed this was the only surviving `1 (Negligible) to 5 (Critical)` scale-field (the sole other `(Critical)` hit, [`risk/annex-ai-risk-methodology.md`](../../risk/annex-ai-risk-methodology.md):104 "score at 25 (Critical)", is the rating-top at score 25, correct).
- **F3 (warning, C7 stale spec)**: [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) (`1.16.2` to `1.16.3`) §6 narrative for gate 48 said the gate "checks two things across the corpus" and described only code-validity and title-match. After the #308 (S4 section-aware cross-catalogue title check) and #309 (S5 bare-domain-code check) enhancements, the linter ([`tools/lint-ccm-aicm-citations.py`](../../tools/lint-ccm-aicm-citations.py)) performs four checks. The linter's own module docstring (lines 22-72) already described all four; only the spec was stale. The §6 prose is rewritten to describe all four checks (code validity, section-aware title match, cross-catalogue title, bare domain code) and closes with a note that the §6 description is kept in step with the linter's module docstring.

### Changed

- [`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md) regenerated to reflect the two bumped documents' Version/Date.
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md) sweep cursor advanced 37 to 38 (gate 45 / gate-36 TodoStaleness).
- [`.working/validate-sweeps/history.md`](../validate-sweeps/history.md) Sweep 38 row added (`2.0.30` to `2.0.31`); per-iteration detail [`.working/validate-sweeps/2026-06-24-sweep38-iter1.md`](../validate-sweeps/2026-06-24-sweep38-iter1.md) written.

### Verification

- `tools/run_all_audits.sh` 48/48 on the committed state; `tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh` green against the merge base.
- F2 (note, C4 inferred-as-verified): #310's CHANGELOG "every risk-scoring document now agrees with the canonical scale" was contradicted by F1; the #310 entry is not retroactively rewritten (CHANGELOG entries are append-only history), and the claim becomes accurate once F1 lands. No separate edit; documented here and in the Sweep 38 detail file.
- Apply-time orchestrator re-read independently confirmed F1 (read `ai/register-ai-risk.md` schema table + corpus-wide `5 (Critical)` grep) and F3 (read spec §6 line 131 + linter docstring; confirmed docstring current and spec stale) before routing. Subagent B: 0 findings. All 9 pre-flight candidates dismissed.

### Discipline observation

The two findings share a shape with prior sweeps' catches: an enhancement or harmonization PR that updated its named target surfaces but missed a structurally-parallel surface (F1, a sibling document outside #310's enumerated set; F3, the spec prose that #308/#309 left un-touched while updating the linter and its docstring). Both are the find-every-carrier / multi-surface-incompleteness class. The orchestrator workers were treated as research: each finding was re-read against the live file before the fix, and the corpus-wide grep for F1 confirmed no further carriers.

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.286, PR #308

**S4: gate 48 section-aware + cross-catalogue title check.** Closes TODO §4.5 S4 (surfaced by PR #299's `/validate-pr`, where the union-dict title check let the I&S-07 cross-catalogue confusion through and the per-PR sweep caught it by hand).

Added Check 3 (`ccm-title-cross-catalogue`): a `_divergent_controls()` table records, per code whose CCM and AICM titles differ by a distinctive content word, the words unique to each catalogue; a row under a CCM/AICM section carrying the OTHER catalogue's distinctive word (and none of THIS catalogue's) is flagged. - Gate 48 stays clean on the live corpus (0 findings across 377 files) after the change, so the section-aware lookup introduces no false positives (CCM_V41 and ALL_TITLES differ only for I&S-07 / IAM-11, both of which still share content words with the conservative check).

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.279, PR #301

**Sweep 36 (`/validate`) close-out: completing the CSA CCM/AICM citation residuals that PR #298's token-scoped reconciliation left behind.** Sweep 36 (the `/resume` loop-break corpus-wide `/validate`, the compensating control for handoff PR #300) dispatched all three subagents. Subagent C (audit-programme integrity) was clean: gate 48 is correctly wired across all four surfaces and the reference module matches the catalogues (207/207 CCM, 247/247 AICM). Subagents A and B, plus the orchestrator's apply-time grep, surfaced one dominant in-window finding-class: PR #298's reconciliation claimed "zero residual invalid codes", but its validator and gate 48 are **token-scoped** (they check `<DOMAIN>-<NN>` tokens and `| CODE | title |` rows), so they were structurally blind to **bare domain-code mentions** (in framework-coverage family lists, the glossary, and the domain-by-domain reverse crosswalk) and to framework **version-currency strings**. The orchestrator's grep found more than the subagents reported (the numbered `GOV`/`AUD` crosswalk in the compliance audit-management policy and the `EKM` row in the reverse crosswalk), reinforcing the incomplete-mechanical-sweep lesson: a bulk fix must re-grep every adjacent surface, not trust a phrasing-specific search.

All domain-code corrections are grounded in the authoritative CSA catalogues (CCM v4.1.0, AICM v1.1.0), used in the session scratchpad only (no-redistribution licence) via the in-repo fair-use index [`tools/ccm_aicm_reference.py`](../../tools/ccm_aicm_reference.py). The catalogue confirms: the v4.0 `IVS` domain was renamed to `I&S` ("Infrastructure Security") in v4.1.0; there is no `GOV` domain (governance is `GRC`), no `NET` domain (network controls are in `I&S`), no `EKM` domain (cryptography/key management is `CEK`), and `AUD` is the v3 code for what is `A&A` in v4.1.

### Fixed
- [`compliance/policy-compliance-and-audit-management.md`](../../compliance/policy-compliance-and-audit-management.md) (`1.0.2` → `1.0.3`): the CSA-frameworks reference and the compliance-mapping table's CSA column used fabricated/superseded codes: `GOV 01 to 03` / `GOV 04` / `GOV 05` / `GOV 06` corrected to `GRC 01 to 03` / `GRC 04` / `GRC 05` / `GRC 06` (governance is the GRC domain), and `AUD 01 to 04` / `AUD 05` corrected to `A&A 01 to 04` / `A&A 05` (audit/assurance is the A&A domain). The CSA-frameworks line `(GOV, AUD, AICM)` corrected to `(GRC, A&A, AICM)`. Space-format codes were retained to match the table's existing `AIS 01 to 03` / `STA 05` style.
- [`governance/matrix-reverse-framework-control-crosswalk.md`](../../governance/matrix-reverse-framework-control-crosswalk.md) (`1.0.2` → `1.0.3`): the domain-by-domain crosswalk listed three superseded codes: `EKM` → `CEK` (and name refreshed to "Cryptography, Encryption, and Key Management"), `GOV` → `GRC`, and `IVS` → `I&S` (name refreshed to "Infrastructure Security").
- [`operations/framework-it-service-management.md`](../../operations/framework-it-service-management.md) (`1.0.0` → `1.0.1`): "SEF and GOV Domains" → "SEF and GRC Domains".
- [`governance/register-glossary.md`](../../governance/register-glossary.md) (`1.4.0` → `1.4.1`): the `IVS` glossary entry ("Infrastructure and Virtualization Security") replaced by an `I&S` entry ("Infrastructure Security", with a note that it was renamed from the v4.0 IVS domain), repositioned alphabetically beside the other CCM-domain entry (IPY).
- [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md) (`1.27.33` → `1.27.34`): two "CSA CCM IVS" Frameworks-column references → "CSA CCM I&S".
- [`compliance/matrix-grc-compliance-alignment.md`](../../compliance/matrix-grc-compliance-alignment.md) (`1.1.1` → `1.1.2`): the CCM family list "...LOG, IVS, NET, CEK..." corrected to "...LOG, I&S, CEK..." (IVS renamed to I&S; the fabricated NET removed since network controls are in I&S).
- [`dev-security/standard-api-security.md`](../../dev-security/standard-api-security.md) (`0.0.6` → `0.0.7`): CCM cross-walk "AIS, IAM, IVS, LOG" → "AIS, IAM, I&S, LOG".
- [`dev-security/standard-cloud-hardening-baseline-aws.md`](../../dev-security/standard-cloud-hardening-baseline-aws.md) (`0.0.5` → `0.0.6`), [`dev-security/standard-cloud-hardening-baseline-gcp.md`](../../dev-security/standard-cloud-hardening-baseline-gcp.md) (`0.0.5` → `0.0.6`), [`dev-security/standard-cloud-hardening-baseline-azure.md`](../../dev-security/standard-cloud-hardening-baseline-azure.md) (`0.0.5` → `0.0.6`): CCM control-matrix row "IAM, IVS, LOG, GRC" → "IAM, I&S, LOG, GRC".
- [`security/policy-information-security.md`](../../security/policy-information-security.md) (`1.3.4` → `1.3.5`): "aligned to CSA CCM IVS controls" → "aligned to CSA CCM I&S controls".
- [`supply-chain/procedure-third-party-ai-due-diligence.md`](../../supply-chain/procedure-third-party-ai-due-diligence.md) (`1.0.2` → `1.0.3`): three "AICM v1.0.3" version-currency strings (synthesis source, checklist item 14, references list) → "AICM v1.1".
- [`dev-security/standard-security-baseline-and-standards-reference.md`](../../dev-security/standard-security-baseline-and-standards-reference.md) (`1.1.2` → `1.1.3`): the Layer-2 frameworks table's AICM version "v1.0.3" → "v1.1.0" (CCM was already correct at v4.1.0 in the row above).
- [`dev-security/claude-rules/README.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/README.md) (pack `1.49.4` → `1.49.5`): the "Cloud security alliance" external-reference section listed CCM at "v4.0" and AICM at "v1.0.3" with "13 AI-specific security controls"; corrected to CCM "v4.1.0" and AICM "v1.1.0" with "247 controls across 18 domains (CCM's 17 plus the AI-specific Model Development and Security (MDS) domain)". The "13" was the MDS-domain subset, not the full AICM scope. Pack version-history row added.

### Surfaced for maintainer review (out-of-window)
- [`operations/standard-physical-security-of-it-infrastructure.md`](../../operations/standard-physical-security-of-it-infrastructure.md):108 cites "I&S-03: Infrastructure and Virtualization Security: Physical Security", but the catalogue title of I&S-03 is "Network Security", and physical/datacenter security is the `DCS` domain. This looks like a control mis-citation (correct replacement likely a DCS control), but the right fix needs judgment, so it is left for morning review rather than guess-fixed. (Two operations files, this one and [`operations/standard-cloud-security-configuration-baseline.md`](../../operations/standard-cloud-security-configuration-baseline.md):195, also carry the old "Infrastructure and Virtualization Security" domain *name* alongside correct I&S codes; that softer domain-name-currency refresh is bundled into the same morning-review note.)

Refreshed the session-resume metadata and the last-validation-sweep cursor to Sweep 36.

- All 48 audit gates pass on the committed state (`tools/run_all_audits.sh`); [`taxonomy.yml`](../../taxonomy.yml), [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md), and [`docs/maturity-scorecard.md`](../../docs/maturity-scorecard.md) regenerated for the per-document version bumps. A corpus-wide standalone-token grep for all eight known-bad codes (`GOV`/`IVS`/`NET`/`GRM`/`AUD`/`EKM`/`MOS`/`DSI`) was run to confirm completeness; `MOS`/`DSI`/`GRM` have zero live CCM-context occurrences.

### Sweep 36 record
- Subagents dispatched: A (recent-PR deep review), B (corpus-wide stale-reference sweep), C (audit-programme integrity). C clean. Full record in [`.working/validate-sweeps/2026-06-24-sweep36-iter1.md`](2026-06-24-sweep36-iter1.md); history row added to [`.working/validate-sweeps/history.md`](history.md).

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.278, PR #300

**Session-closing handoff PR for the 2026-06-24 CCM/AICM session, carrying the one PR #299 `/validate-pr` finding-fix and setting up an authorized overnight mission.** The session resumed via `/resume`, ran Sweep 35, and on its findings pivoted to a maintainer-directed CCM/AICM citation-accuracy programme: #298 (corpus-wide reconciliation grounded in the maintainer-supplied authoritative CSA CCM v4.1.0 / AICM v1.1.0 catalogues) and #299 (gate 48 + full gap-register title alignment). This handoff lands working state on `main` as a green merge so the next `/resume` rebuilds from `main`.

### Fixed
- [`dev-security/register-compliance-controls-and-gap-register.md`](../../dev-security/register-compliance-controls-and-gap-register.md) (`1.0.2` → `1.0.3`): **PR #299 `/validate-pr` finding (A-299-1)**. The I&S-07 row sits under the "CSA CCM v4.1" section but carried "Migration to Hosted Environments", which is the **AICM v1.1** title; the CCM v4.1 title is "Migration to Cloud Environments". I&S-07 is the only control whose title diverges between the two catalogues and appears in the register; #299's alignment used the AICM-wins union dict and picked the AICM value for a CCM-section row. Corrected to the CCM value. The durable fix (a section-aware gate-48 title check) is queued as TODO S4.

### Changed
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): added **S4** (gate-48 section-aware title check) under §4.5; refreshed the session-resume metadata.
- Refreshed [`.working/session-handoff.md`](session-handoff.md) to the post-#299 state and, per maintainer authorization (2026-06-24), set up the **overnight mission**: the next session is an autonomous ~8-hour run whose goal is to close all XS and S items and as many Medium items as possible from TODO for morning review, following the overnight-work protocol and every per-PR QA discipline (no abbreviation). The handoff enumerates the effort-ordered work-list and the autonomy guidance (proceed on recorded dispositions; sensible-default-and-flag for new bounded choices; defer costly/ambiguous choices to morning; no fabrication on egress-gated items).

### Verification
- Carries the batched **#299 `/validate-pr`** record ([`.working/validate-pr/2026-06-24-PR-299.md`](validate-pr/2026-06-24-PR-299.md), 1 in-window error, fixed here) + history row and the **#299 `/retro`** row ([`.working/improvement-log.md`](improvement-log.md): the 4th-occurrence incomplete-mechanical-sweep pattern, with the gate-48 section-aware enhancement as the proposed durable fix).
- Per the handoff-PR exception (CLAUDE.md PR-workflow step 5a), this PR **skips its own trailing `/validate-pr` + `/retro`** (loop-break, no terminating next PR at a session boundary); the compensating control is the corpus-wide **Sweep 36** the next `/resume` runs first. The skip is recorded inline in the [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](validate-pr/history.md) #300 row.

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.277, PR #299

**New gate 48 (CSA CCM/AICM citation-accuracy audit) backed by a fair-use catalogue reference, plus the full v4.1/v1.1 title alignment of the compliance-controls gap register (the PR #298 `/validate-pr` finding, expanded).** The follow-up to PR #298's reconciliation: a mechanical backstop so the CCM/AICM citation-accuracy class cannot recur. PR #298's `/validate-pr` found the gap register's I&S and AICM/MDS control titles were mis-attributed; building the gate's title check revealed the mismatch was systematic (the register's control catalogue had been authored against an older CCM version), so the maintainer directed a full alignment of every control row to its authoritative title.

### Added
- **Gate 48, [`tools/lint-ccm-aicm-citations.py`](../../tools/lint-ccm-aicm-citations.py)**: validates, corpus-wide, that every `<DOMAIN>-<NN>` token in the CCM/AICM family names a real domain and an in-range control number (catching superseded/fabricated domains and out-of-range numbers), and that control-listing rows (`| <CODE> | <title> |`) whose cited title shares no content word with the catalogue title are flagged as mis-attributed (a conservative check that tolerates the corpus's localized/abbreviated titles). Corpus-internal `MODEL-GOV-NN` / `AI-GOV-NN` identifiers and other frameworks' codes are not policed. Wired into all four audit-programme surfaces ([`.github/workflows/quality.yml`](../../.github/workflows/quality.yml), [`tools/run_all_audits.sh`](../../tools/run_all_audits.sh), [`.pre-commit-config.yaml`](../../.pre-commit-config.yaml), [`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) §6 row 48 + the content-drift-defence category + the gate-description paragraph) with a five-case regression fixture in [`tests/test_linters.py`](../../tests/test_linters.py). Gate count 47 to 48.
- **[`tools/ccm_aicm_reference.py`](../../tools/ccm_aicm_reference.py)**: the gate's authoritative reference, a fair-use citation index of domain codes, control-ID ranges, and canonical control titles derived from the CSA-published CCM v4.1.0 and AICM v1.1.0 catalogues. It carries none of those catalogues' normative content (no control specifications, guidelines, or CAIQ), so it is an index for citation verification, not a redistribution of the matrices (which carry a no-redistribution clause). The module docstring records provenance, the fair-use basis, and the refresh procedure.

### Changed
- **[`dev-security/register-compliance-controls-and-gap-register.md`](../../dev-security/register-compliance-controls-and-gap-register.md) (`1.0.1` to `1.0.2`)**: every control-listing title aligned to its authoritative CCM v4.1.0 / AICM v1.1.0 title (54 rows, plus the 21 I&S/MDS/GRC-09 rows already corrected in PR #298 as the `/validate-pr` finding), and the stale `(IVS)` section header corrected to `Infrastructure security (I&S)`. The register's control catalogue had been authored against an older CCM version (its DCS, CEK, and UEM sections were notably shifted), so titles such as `MDS-01 "Model Inventory"` (authoritative: "Training Pipeline Security") and `DCS-01 "Off-site Equipment Disposal"` (authoritative: "Physical and Environmental Security Policy and Procedures") were systematically wrong. NOTE for future review: the register's control *selection* (which control IDs it lists per domain) was made against the old numbering and may warrant a separate intent review; this PR corrected the titles to match the IDs, not the selection.
- **[`governance/specification-audit-programme.md`](../../governance/specification-audit-programme.md) (`1.16.1` to `1.16.2`)**: §6 inventory gains gate 48; the content-drift-defence category and the gate-description paragraph document it; gate count updated. Added by name to the gate's meta-document exemption per §3.4.
- **[`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md)**: gate count 47 to 48; DD-12 part (a) note reworded (the fabricated `NET` domain rows, resolved in #298).

### Verification
- All 48 gates pass on the committed state, including the new gate 48 (0 findings across 380 files), gate 35 (four-surface parity for gate 48), gate 36 (the new five-case regression fixture), and gate 39 (gate count 48). The gate was run corpus-wide and confirmed to flag the gap-register mismatches before the alignment and zero after. `taxonomy.yml` / `docs/portal.md` / `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated for the version bumps.
- **Carries the batched PR #298 `/validate-pr` record** ([`.working/validate-pr/2026-06-24-PR-298.md`](../validate-pr/2026-06-24-PR-298.md), 2 in-window warnings, fixed here) **and `/retro` row** ([`.working/improvement-log.md`](../improvement-log.md): the 3rd-occurrence incomplete-mechanical-sweep pattern, whose proposed improvement, a code-to-title gate, is exactly what this PR ships).

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.276, PR #298

**Sweep 35 (`/validate`) close-out plus a maintainer-directed corpus-wide CSA CCM / AICM citation-accuracy reconciliation, grounded in the authoritative CSA-published catalogues.** The `/resume` loop-break Sweep 35 surfaced two findings (A-1: a `GRM`→`GRC` stale CCM domain code in the cross-framework matrix; B-1: a `12 months`→`7 years` stale AI-log retention figure). Apply-time verification of A-1 surfaced an adjacent invalid `GOV domain` reference, and a corpus-wide grep exposed a **systemic CCM/AICM citation-accuracy problem**. The maintainer supplied the authoritative **CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4.1.0** and **AI Controls Matrix v1.1.0** catalogues (XLSX + guidance PDFs) and directed a full reconciliation. The catalogues were used as the definitive reference but, per their explicit no-redistribution licence (CCM/AICM "may not be redistributed ... may not be modified"), are **not committed** to this CC BY-SA 4.0 repository; they were parsed in the session scratchpad only, and a fair-use reference of the factual domain codes + control-ID ranges will back a follow-up validation gate. A validator built from the catalogues confirmed the complete defect set and **zero residual defects** after the fixes.

### Fixed
- **GOV-NN cited as CSA CCM v4.1 (fabricated domain; ~9 documents).** Neither CCM v4.1 nor AICM v1.1 has a `GOV` domain, governance is `GRC` in both (CCM: GRC-01..08; AICM extends to GRC-15). `GOV-01..08` (governance base) corrected to `GRC-01..08` in [`compliance/policy-legal-and-regulatory-compliance.md`](../../compliance/policy-legal-and-regulatory-compliance.md) and [`dev-security/standard-security-baseline-and-standards-reference.md`](../../dev-security/standard-security-baseline-and-standards-reference.md). The `GOV-09`/`GOV-10` citations carried **fabricated control titles** ("Sustainability and Responsible Technology", "Governance and Continuous Improvement", "Governance Metrics and Monitoring", "Governance and digital trust") that match no real CCM/AICM control; all six rows removed (each table already cited the genuinely-correct COBIT MEA01 reference) from [`governance/framework-metrics-monitoring-and-performance-reporting.md`](../../governance/framework-metrics-monitoring-and-performance-reporting.md), [`governance/framework-governance-performance-and-improvement.md`](../../governance/framework-governance-performance-and-improvement.md), [`governance/framework-continuous-assurance-and-improvement.md`](../../governance/framework-continuous-assurance-and-improvement.md), [`governance/register-digital-trust-and-assurance-metrics.md`](../../governance/register-digital-trust-and-assurance-metrics.md), [`compliance/procedure-control-testing.md`](../../compliance/procedure-control-testing.md), and [`governance/framework-sustainability-and-responsible-technology.md`](../../governance/framework-sustainability-and-responsible-technology.md). Bare `GOV domain` refs removed from [`governance/matrix-cross-framework-alignment.md`](../../governance/matrix-cross-framework-alignment.md):64 and [`risk/policy-enterprise-governance-and-risk-management.md`](../../risk/policy-enterprise-governance-and-risk-management.md):136.
- **GRM → GRC (Sweep 35 A-1).** Six cells in [`governance/matrix-cross-framework-alignment.md`](../../governance/matrix-cross-framework-alignment.md) under the "CSA CCM v4.1" column used the v3.0.1 `GRM` domain code; corrected to `GRC`. Same error class as #294's FR-155, which this PR completes for the matrix surface FR-155 did not enumerate.
- **IVS → I&S (34 occurrences, corpus + pack).** CCM v4.1.0 renamed the Infrastructure & Virtualization Security domain code from `IVS` (v4.0) to `I&S` (controls I&S-01..09, titles preserved 1:1). All `IVS-NN` citations corrected to `I&S-NN` across 11 corpus documents and the distributed pack ([`dev-security/claude-rules/ai/mcp-security.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/ai/mcp-security.md), [`core/cryptography.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/core/cryptography.md), [`pipeline/cicd-gates.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/pipeline/cicd-gates.md), and the byte-identical [`.claude/rules/cicd-gates.md`](../../.claude/rules/cicd-gates.md) mirror).
- **NET → I&S (not a CCM domain).** CCM v4.1 has no `NET` domain; network controls live in I&S (I&S-03 Network Security, I&S-06 Segmentation, I&S-08 Network Architecture, I&S-09 Network Defense). [`compliance/matrix-grc-compliance-alignment.md`](../../compliance/matrix-grc-compliance-alignment.md):103 `NET-01..06` → `I&S-03, I&S-06, I&S-08, I&S-09`; [`dev-security/standard-security-baseline-and-standards-reference.md`](../../dev-security/standard-security-baseline-and-standards-reference.md):227 `NET-01 to 09` → `I&S-03, I&S-08, I&S-09`. **Resolves TODO DD-12 part (a)** (which had targeted the wrong `IVS` code, itself a v4.0 artefact).
- **IPY-05 (out of range).** The Interoperability & Portability domain has exactly four controls (IPY-01..04) in both CCM v4.1 and AICM v1.1. [`supply-chain/standard-cloud-exit-and-data-portability.md`](../../supply-chain/standard-cloud-exit-and-data-portability.md) "IPY-01 through IPY-05" → "IPY-01 through IPY-04"; [`supply-chain/template-supplier-offboarding-evidence.md`](../../supply-chain/template-supplier-offboarding-evidence.md) "IPY-04, IPY-05" → "IPY-04 (data portability contractual obligations)".
- **AI-log retention 12 months → 7 years (Sweep 35 B-1).** [`ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-access-and-agent-permissions.md):193, the agent tool-invocation audit-log retention, the fourth carrier DD-6/7 (#294) missed; aligned to the canonical 7-year AI decision/detection-log figure with a cross-reference to [`governance/register-data-retention-schedule.md`](../../governance/register-data-retention-schedule.md). A corpus-wide grep confirmed it was the sole remaining stale carrier.

### Changed
- **AICM v1.0.3 → v1.1 (currency).** AICM v1.1.0 was released 2026-06-23. [`governance/register-canonical-citations.md`](../../governance/register-canonical-citations.md) updated (current version v1.1, 2026, v1.0.3 moved to superseded); carriers updated in [`ai/framework-ai-system-audit-certification.md`](../../ai/framework-ai-system-audit-certification.md), [`dev-security/register-compliance-controls-and-gap-register.md`](../../dev-security/register-compliance-controls-and-gap-register.md), [`governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md`](../../governance/register-document-index-and-classification.md), and the [`governance/worklist-citation-verification-batch-q4-canonical-citations.md`](../../governance/worklist-citation-verification-batch-q4-canonical-citations.md) task line.
- **CCM version precision.** Stale `CCM v4.0.12` → `v4.1` in [`compliance/register-compliance-obligations-template.md`](../../compliance/register-compliance-obligations-template.md); loose bare `CCM v4` tightened to `v4.1` for corpus consistency in `supply-chain/standard-supplier-security-and-privacy-assurance.md`, `dev-security/standard-developer-security-requirements.md`, `dev-security/standard-devops-security-requirements.md`, `operations/standard-production-security-requirements.md`, `dev-security/README.md`, `specification-master-project.md`, and the pack [`dev-security/claude-rules/CLAUDE.md`](../../dev-security/claude-rules/CLAUDE.md) (`CSA CCM v4 / AICM v1` → `v4.1 / v1.1`).

### Added
- **GOV-09/10 add-backs** (maintainer-directed: remove the fabrication, then add a real control where one genuinely fits). A&A (Audit & Assurance) rows added to the three assurance/control-testing tables (continuous-assurance: A&A-01/A&A-05; control-testing: A&A-02/A&A-05; digital-trust-assurance-metrics: A&A-02), and an AICM v1.1 responsible-AI row (GRC-10/GRC-11/GRC-12: AI Impact Assessment; Bias and Fairness; Ethics Committee) added to the sustainability/responsible-technology framework. No add-back on the two pure-performance tables (COBIT MEA01 already covers them; no fitting CCM/AICM control exists).

### Verification
- A catalogue-derived validator confirms **0 residual** invalid CCM/AICM-family control codes corpus-wide (the only remaining `GOV-NN`/`NET` matches are the corpus-internal `MODEL-GOV`/`AI-GOV` IDs and the resolved DD-12(a) TODO note). Per-document `Version` + `Date` bumped on all 33 edited corpus documents in this commit; pack `1.49.3 → 1.49.4` (citation fixes in pack rules); `taxonomy.yml`, `docs/portal.md`, `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated. `tools/run_all_audits.sh` and `tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh` results recorded at commit time.
- **Folds in the Sweep 35 close-out** (history row + [`.working/validate-sweeps/2026-06-24-sweep35-iter1.md`](../validate-sweeps/2026-06-24-sweep35-iter1.md)); A-1 and B-1 are fixed above. This is a substantive non-handoff PR; its own `/validate-pr` + `/retro` run after merge and batch into the next PR (the follow-up gate PR).

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.275, PR #297

**Session-closing handoff PR for the 2026-06-24 resume session.** The session resumed via `/resume`, ran the loop-break Sweep 34 `/validate` (3 findings → #293), then shipped #294 (PR-H), #295 (metrics refresh), and #296 (PR-I), then wound down on the session-continuation SOP's large-series clause: the next queued item (the relocation bundle PR-J = R1/R2/R3, an atomic-or-break 8-move series a prior session had also deferred per CHANGELOG-detailed:1358) is deferred to a focused next session with a full ripple map now recorded in the handoff. No degradation occurred (four clean merges; the only apply-time misses were the steady-state missed-parallel-carrier class, both caught by `/validate-pr`).

### Fixed
- [`operations/register-asset-inventory.md`](../../operations/register-asset-inventory.md) (`1.0.3` → `1.0.4`): **PR #296 `/validate-pr` finding (FR-160 third surface)**. The asset-criticality tiers read "Tier 2: Essential" / "Tier 3: Important", the old DR-plan labels that PR #296 (FR-160) re-labelled to the SLM-canonical "Business Essential" / "Standard". FR-160 aligned the DR plan and the SLM standard but missed this third carrier (same four-tier model, near-identical example systems). Aligned to "Tier 2: Business Essential" / "Tier 3: Standard"; a corpus-wide grep confirms all three carriers (DR plan, SLM standard, asset inventory) now agree and no other carrier of the old labels remains.

### Changed
- Carries the batched **#296 `/validate-pr`** record ([`.working/validate-pr/2026-06-24-PR-296.md`](2026-06-24-PR-296.md)) + history row (1 warning, the FR-160 third surface, fixed here) and the **#296 `/retro`** row ([`.working/improvement-log.md`](improvement-log.md): the 2nd-occurrence missed-parallel-carrier signal). Refreshes [`.working/session-handoff.md`](session-handoff.md) to the post-#296 state, including the full R1/R2/R3 relocation ripple map (PR #116 precedent; per-relocation load-path edits and index/README deletions) for the next session, and the integrity-tooling phase as the post-XS/S priority. Rotates the asset-inventory FR-160 third-surface item to DONE.

### Verification
- Per the handoff-PR exception (CLAUDE.md PR-workflow step 5a), this PR skips its own trailing `/validate-pr` + `/retro` (loop-break, no terminating next PR at a session boundary); the compensating control is the corpus-wide Sweep 35 the next `/resume` runs first. The skip is recorded inline in the [`.working/validate-pr/history.md`](validate-pr/history.md) #297 row per the documented-exception requirement.
- `tools/run_all_audits.sh` and `tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh` results recorded at commit time; `taxonomy.yml` + `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated for the asset-inventory version bump. Corpus-wide grep confirmed the DR-tier-label harmonization is now complete across all three carriers.

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.274, PR #296

**PR-I of the XS/S batch: a cross-document-consistency bundle (FR-149, FR-150, FR-151, FR-159, FR-160, FR-165; FR-12 found already-closed), plus the two PR #294 `/validate-pr` retention-ripple fixes and the PR #295 README-Date fix.** A research worker scoped each item against the live corpus; the orchestrator re-read every target at apply-time and authored the final prose. Four directional choices were maintainer-confirmed (FR-149 genericize; FR-150 soften to a hedge; FR-151 severity-table-canonical P1=5 days; FR-160 align DR to SLM labels); three were maintainer-announced defaults (FR-12 stale-line deletion, FR-159 generator-Overview placement, FR-165 footnote-not-relabel).

### Changed
- [`ai/standard-ai-and-agentic-development-security.md`](../../ai/standard-ai-and-agentic-development-security.md) (`1.8.2` → `1.8.3`): **FR-149**. §22 said the adversarial suite "must cover five categories", but the [`ai/guide-ai-adversarial-test-reference.md`](../../ai/guide-ai-adversarial-test-reference.md) has six `### Category` headings, the 6th of which is an explicit application-specific *template* (and the guide's own §B4 refers to "B1.1 through B1.5", treating five as the fixed set). Genericized to "must cover the categories defined in the AI Adversarial Test Reference" so the count cannot drift; the naive five→six fix would have over-counted the template as a required category and required a second edit to §B4.
- [`privacy/framework-childrens-data.md`](../../privacy/framework-childrens-data.md) (`1.0.5` → `1.0.6`): **FR-150**. The Japan APPI row claimed "Under 18 generally, under 15 stricter", a specific two-tier age unsupported by the corpus's Japan annex, backed by no canonical citation, and not how APPI works (it sets no GDPR-Article-8-style statutory consent age). Softened to "No fixed statutory numeric age; guardian involvement expected for minors under PPC guidance | Personal Information Protection Commission guidance applies", mirroring the row's existing PIPEDA hedge. External verification was egress-gated; the soften is egress-independent and removes the unsupported claim rather than asserting a new unverified figure.
- [`resilience/procedure-cross-domain-incident-coordination.md`](../../resilience/procedure-cross-domain-incident-coordination.md) (`1.1.4` → `1.1.5`): **FR-151**. The coordination-lifecycle table (§coordination) stated the multi-domain PIR was due "within 10 business days of closure for P1 and P2 events", contradicting the Severity-rules-across-streams table (P1 = 5 business days, P2 = 10, P3 = 15). Fixed the lifecycle cell to defer to the per-severity table; the severity table is canonical (it preserves the P1-tightest graduation), so P1's PIR deadline is 5 days, not a blanket 10.
- [`resilience/plan-it-disaster-recovery.md`](../../resilience/plan-it-disaster-recovery.md) (`1.3.2` → `1.3.3`): **FR-160**. The DR plan's recovery tiers labelled Tier 2 "Essential" and Tier 3 "Important", diverging from the [`operations/standard-service-level-management.md`](../../operations/standard-service-level-management.md) service-classification tiers ("Business Essential", "Standard") for the same four-tier construct (Tier 1 "Mission Critical" and Tier 4 "Non-Critical" already agreed). Aligned the DR plan's two labels to the SLM standard, which is the service-classification authority. Tier references elsewhere in the DR plan use "Tier N", so nothing broke.
- [`tools/build-portal.py`](../../tools/build-portal.py): **FR-159**. The generated portal's Overview pointed only to the document index, not the glossary. The generator's Overview paragraph now also points to [`governance/register-glossary.md`](../../governance/register-glossary.md) ("Glossary and Acronym Index"); [`docs/portal.md`](../../docs/portal.md) regenerated (the pointer landed at the Overview). The fix is in the generator, not the generated file (artefact-and-branch discipline).
- [`governance/standard-records-retention-and-destruction.md`](../../governance/standard-records-retention-and-destruction.md) (`1.4.3` → `1.4.4`): **PR #294 `/validate-pr` F1 (warning)**. The IT/Security retention summary read "1 to 5 years" but PR #294's new AI-decision-logs register row (an IT/Security-class 7-year entry) outdated the upper bound. Widened to "1 to 7 years, e.g. access logs 1 year, security incident records 5 years, AI decision and detection logs 7 years".
- [`security/standard-logging-and-monitoring.md`](../../security/standard-logging-and-monitoring.md) (`1.4.2` → `1.4.3`): **PR #294 `/validate-pr` F2 (note)**. The §4.1 "for example" log-retention list omitted the new 7-year AI tier (§6 governs AI-system logging). Appended ", AI decision and detection logs 7 years" to the example list.

### Fixed (working-state)
- [`.working/validate-sweeps/2026-06-22-sweep22-iter1.md`](../validate-sweeps/2026-06-22-sweep22-iter1.md): **FR-165**. The Termination paragraph (`:92`) claimed the #231-#241 `/validate-pr` rows "now read 'deferred to Sweep 22 close-out…' rather than 'abbreviated spot-check'", but the rows were deliberately **retained** verbatim (with only #240/#241 annotated and #241 made the reconciliation entry). Added a dated correction footnote that preserves the frozen text and explicitly records that relabelling the rows was rejected (doing so would launder the honest abbreviation-discipline record the trust-recovery escalation exists to preserve). Frozen-archive accuracy fix; not relabelling the history rows. The TODO/brief said "#221-#239"; the actual affected range is #231-#241.
- [`README.md`](../../README.md): **PR #295 `/validate-pr` note**. README Date bumped 2026-06-23 → 2026-06-24 to track the version bump (it had lagged on #295's version-only edit).

### Removed
- [`TODO.md`](../../TODO.md): **FR-12** deleted as a stale entry. Apply-time re-read of [`risk/procedure-risk-register.md`](../../risk/procedure-risk-register.md) Step 8 confirmed the cross-document harmonisation FR-12 tracked was already closed by PR #239 (the procedure now references the canonical six treatment options + the Treatment Status workflow). No corpus change; DONE already records #239's closure.

### Verification
- `tools/run_all_audits.sh` clean (all 47 gates) before and after; `lint-language.py` clean over the new prose. `taxonomy.yml`, `docs/portal.md`, `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated for the six per-doc bumps and the portal generator change; the FR-159 glossary pointer confirmed present in the regenerated portal Overview.
- Apply-time verification (orchestrator re-read): FR-12 closure confirmed in `procedure-risk-register.md` Step 8; FR-149 confirmed against the guide's six headings + §B4 "B1.1-B1.5"; FR-151 confirmed against the severity table (P1=5/P2=10/P3=15); FR-160 confirmed against the SLM §1.2 tier labels; the FR-159 glossary target confirmed as `register-glossary.md` (the "Glossary and Acronym Index", distinct from the "Key Terms and Definitions Register"). FR-150's APPI position was NOT asserted from general knowledge (egress-gated); the soften removes the claim instead.
- Seven items rotated TODO → DONE (the six FRs plus the two #294 retention items folded into the bundle; FR-12 deleted as already-closed). The pre-existing ERC-expansion inconsistency remains queued. Carries the batched PR #295 `/validate-pr` record + history row and `/retro` row.

## 2026-06-24, Library Version 2026.06.273, PR #295

`.working/` for local project: refreshed the [`.working/hallucination-metrics.md`](hallucination-metrics.md) summary table (TODO §4.9), frozen since PR #176, by recomputing the aggregate counts from the live catches/escapes logs. The recompute surfaced two clarifications recorded inline in the file: (1) the catches log had accumulated **orchestrator-side discipline catches** (multi-surface incompleteness, the C-9 mandatory-QA skip, registry/docstring misses) distinct from the file's defined **worker-draft-error** metric, now counted on a separate row so they do not inflate the worker-error rate; (2) the per-event logs run through Sweep 30 / #275, so catches since then (#277-#294) are summarized from the `/retro` ([`improvement-log.md`](improvement-log.md)) and `/validate-pr` ([`validate-pr/`](validate-pr/)) records rather than re-logged. Recomputed figures: ~13 worker-draft apply-time catches, 2 worker-draft shipped escapes (both pre-#167, so the recent-window escape rate is ~0), ~9 orchestrator-side discipline catches. Trend read: worker hallucination is controlled (the 1.1.0 worker-brief template guards the most recent class at source); the active failure class is the orchestrator-side multi-surface family driving the TODO §4.8 codification backlog. Closes TODO §4.9 (rotated to [`.working/DONE.md`](DONE.md)); the P4 subsection count is updated to 9. Carries the batched PR #294 `/validate-pr` record + history row (2 in-window retention-ripple findings, fixes bundled to PR-I) and the #294 `/retro` row. Working-state only; no adopter-facing corpus content changed. Library `2026.06.272` to `2026.06.273`; README `1.9.143` to `1.9.144`.

## 2026-06-23, Library Version 2026.06.272, PR #294

**PR-H of the XS/S batch: a seven-item governance/registers + ESG bundle (FR-120, FR-155, FR-53, FR-109, DD-6/DD-7, FR-75, FR-76), built as one PR per maintainer direction; B2 deferred on egress.** A research worker surfaced each item's defect, fix shape, and directional choices; the orchestrator re-read every target file at apply-time and authored the final prose. Three directional choices were maintainer-confirmed (FR-120 soften the standards attribution; FR-75/76 qualitative ESG with no invented numbers; B2 defer because EDPB/WP29 citation verification needs web egress that is policy-blocked). The worker also corrected the FR-155 TODO premise (only one of the three cited files carried the stale id) and surfaced a pre-existing ERC-expansion inconsistency (routed to TODO, not fixed here).

### Changed
- [`governance/policy-exception-and-risk-acceptance-management.md`](../../governance/policy-exception-and-risk-acceptance-management.md) (`1.3.2` → `1.3.3`): **FR-120**. §3.7.1's attribution of the 180-day baseline overstated that NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5 CA-6 has "semi-annual control-monitoring cadences" and paired ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 as if it prescribes one. Softened: the text now states that neither CA-6 (organisation-defined monitoring frequency) nor Clause 9.2 ("internal audit at planned intervals") prescribes a fixed interval, and 180 days is chosen to fall within the semi-annual-to-annual band typical implementations use. Keeps the "180 days is a library convention" framing (the strongest and most accurate stance); reduces the claim rather than adding an unverifiable one.
- [`risk/policy-enterprise-governance-and-risk-management.md`](../../risk/policy-enterprise-governance-and-risk-management.md) (`1.4.4` → `1.4.5`): **FR-155**. The framework-alignment table's CSA CCM v4.1 row read "GRM domain" (the retired CCM v3 governance-domain id); corrected to "GRC domain" (the v4.1 id), keeping the valid BCR/GOV/AIS prefixes. The TODO premise that all three cited files carried the drift was wrong: the standard (`:250`) and methodology (`:183`) already used "GRC"; only the policy carried "GRM", so this is a one-line fix and the other two files were not touched.
- [`specification-ingestion.md`](../../specification-ingestion.md) (`1.7.3` → `1.7.4`): **FR-53 (decided: document the distinction, do not deprecate)**. The metadata block carries both `Classification` and `Confidentiality`, both always "Public" in this corpus, raising a redundancy question. Added a note to the `## Canonical metadata` section's field-convention prose defining the two as independent axes: `Classification` is the library's handling/lifecycle category (Public / Reference Only / Deprecated, per the document-index register), `Confidentiality` is the information-sensitivity tier of the content; they coincide at Public here but a fork could hold a Reference-Only-but-Confidential document. Both fields are retained.
- [`governance/charter-governance-library.md`](../../governance/charter-governance-library.md) (`1.2.0` → `1.2.1`): **FR-109**. The Purpose section's third paragraph packed four ideas into one ~110-word block with a 60-word run-on first sentence and a "usable … usable" repetition. Tightened in place (the maintainer-announced editorial default): the run-on is split into two sentences and the repetition removed, preserving the paragraph and all four ideas.
- [`ai/checklist-ai-algorithmic-compliance.md`](../../ai/checklist-ai-algorithmic-compliance.md) (`1.0.2` → `1.0.3`) and [`governance/register-data-retention-schedule.md`](../../governance/register-data-retention-schedule.md) (`1.0.5` → `1.0.6`): **DD-6/DD-7**. The checklist's C6 row retained AI system logs "for a minimum of 12 months per [the logging standard]", a value the cited logging standard does not actually state (it defers to the retention schedule) and below the canonical AI-log retention. Reconciled up to "a minimum of 7 years per the AI decision and detection logs row in [the data-retention schedule]" (re-pointing the citation to the authoritative register and fixing the broken cross-reference). The retention schedule's §7 AI governance records gains the matching row: "AI decision and detection logs (inputs, outputs, model version, confidence scores) | 7 years | ISO/IEC 42001; EU AI Act Annex IV; AI-decision accountability (longer than the general SIEM event tier)". The 7-year figure and the ISO 42001 / EU AI Act Annex IV basis were already asserted in [`operations/procedure-security-monitoring-and-alert-management.md`](../../operations/procedure-security-monitoring-and-alert-management.md):298, so this propagates an existing corpus-consistent value rather than introducing a new external citation; all three surfaces now converge.
- [`governance/guideline-esg-and-ai-ethics-disclosure.md`](../../governance/guideline-esg-and-ai-ethics-disclosure.md) (`1.0.0` → `1.1.0`): **FR-75**. §1 Materiality stated a double-materiality lens but no threshold/determination method. Added a qualitative double-materiality assessment process (performed at least annually, owned by the GRC Programme Manager with stakeholder input, recorded and retained) with an explicit statement that no single quantitative cut-off is prescribed, since the corpus is organisation-neutral (maintainer-confirmed: qualitative, no invented numbers).
- [`governance/framework-sustainability-and-responsible-technology.md`](../../governance/framework-sustainability-and-responsible-technology.md) (`1.1.1` → `1.2.0`): **FR-76**. §7 had KPIs and a governance table but no escalation triggers. Added a qualitative "Escalation triggers" paragraph: the CIO escalates to the ERC, and to the Board where material, on a KPI missing target for two consecutive periods, a substantiated human-rights due-diligence finding, or a material adverse ESG event; triggers are qualitative by design (each adopter calibrates tolerance bands). Uses the doc's existing ERC term (already in use at §3) and KPI categories; no invented numbers.

### Deferred
- **B2** (add five EDPB/WP29 soft-law citations to `governance/register-canonical-citations.md`): verifying the exact titles, years, and issuing bodies needs web egress to edpb.europa.eu, which returned HTTP 403 (organisation policy denial) for both the research worker and the orchestrator's proxy. Per the no-fabrication discipline, the citations are not added unverified. Deferred to a session with egress (or the maintainer supplies the verified details); the TODO entry records that two of the five (Opinion 05/2014, WP248 rev.01) are Article 29 Working Party documents, not EDPB, and Guidelines 28/2024's title is the least certain.

### Verification
- `tools/run_all_audits.sh` clean (all 47 gates) before and after the edits; `lint-language.py` (inside the suite) clean over the new prose (no em/en dashes, no British `-ise`). `taxonomy.yml`, `docs/portal.md`, and `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated for the per-doc version bumps.
- Apply-time verification (orchestrator re-read): every worker-quoted line confirmed against the live file; the FR-155 single-file scope confirmed by re-reading the standard and methodology (both already "GRC"); the DD-6/DD-7 citation confirmed corpus-consistent against `procedure-...:298` and `standard-records-retention-and-destruction.md:142`; the ERC term confirmed already in use in the sustainability framework (no new first-use acronym introduced). The CA-6 / Clause 9.2 / EU AI Act Annex IV external wording could not be re-fetched (egress 403); the FR-120 fix is claim-reducing (it retracts an over-specific attribution) and the DD-6/7 citation is corpus-internal, so neither adds an unverifiable external assertion.
- Seven items rotated TODO → DONE; B2 annotated deferred-on-egress; the pre-existing ERC-expansion inconsistency added to TODO P3 (no-drop). Carries the batched PR #293 `/validate-pr` record + history row (0 in-window; 1 out-of-window routed) and `/retro` row, plus the Sweep 34 out-of-window finding in TODO, all committed earlier on this branch.

## 2026-06-23, Library Version 2026.06.271, PR #293

**Sweep 34 (`/validate`) close-out: correct a false "the Day-1 floor nests inside Tier 1" claim across two adopter-onboarding surfaces, and reconcile a breach-response role lead-in.** The `/resume` compensating-control corpus-wide `/validate` (Sweep 34, the standing loop-break control for the session-closing handoff PR #292, which skipped its trailing `/validate-pr` + `/retro` per CLAUDE.md PR-workflow step 5a) ran a full three-subagent dispatch over the whole corpus plus the #289-#292 deltas. Subagents B (corpus-wide stale-reference) and C (audit-programme integrity) returned 0 findings; the security-critical FR-153 crypto and FR-90 browser-hardening citations from #291 verified clean and current, and a subagent WebSearch over-correction (PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 "210,000") was refuted at apply-time against the authoritative OWASP source (the corpus's 220,000 is correct). Subagent A (recent-PR deep review) surfaced one defect across two parallel surfaces plus one note, all in-window.

### Fixed
- [`docs/adopter-guide.md`](../../docs/adopter-guide.md) (`1.3.0` → `1.3.1`) and [`docs/template-quickstart.md`](../../docs/template-quickstart.md) (`3.0.2` → `3.0.3`): **Sweep 34 F1/F2 (C4/C3 multi-surface incompleteness, in-window from #290/FR-69)**. Both docs claimed the six-artefact Day-1 floor "includes" / "sits inside" / "is a subset of" the 15-document Tier 1 starter set and that "the three sizes nest rather than compete". This is false: two of the six Day-1-floor artefacts, [`security/policy-acceptable-use.md`](../../security/policy-acceptable-use.md) and [`security/policy-identity-and-access-management.md`](../../security/policy-identity-and-access-management.md), do not appear in the Tier 1 table, so the floor overlaps Tier 1 in only four of its six items and does not nest inside it. The maintainer chose to soften the prose (preserve the deliberately-curated 15-document Tier 1 and its 4-to-6-hour reading time) rather than expand Tier 1 to 17 documents. Both surfaces now state the accurate overlap ("shares four of those six artefacts, while the Day-1 floor additionally names the acceptable-use and identity-and-access-management policies") and describe the three sizes as "complementary starting points rather than competing ones". Fixed in lock-step across the two parallel surfaces.
- [`privacy/procedure-data-protection-and-privacy-breach-response.md`](../../privacy/procedure-data-protection-and-privacy-breach-response.md) (`1.4.12` → `1.4.13`): **Sweep 34 F3 (C6 internal tension, in-window from #291/FR-85, note-level)**. The §4.2 lead-in read "the Data Protection Officer and CISO jointly conduct an initial assessment. The two roles conduct it jointly, but each determination has a named lead", while the table immediately below assigns determinations 5 and 6 to "Data Protection Officer, with Legal", a third contributing role the "two roles" phrasing did not admit. The lead-in now reads "...jointly conduct an initial assessment, drawing in Legal where a determination requires it. Each determination has a named lead", reconciling the prose with the table.

### Changed
- Carries the Sweep 34 history row ([`.working/validate-sweeps/history.md`](../validate-sweeps/history.md), `2.0.26` → `2.0.27`) and the per-iteration detail file [`.working/validate-sweeps/2026-06-23-sweep34-iter1.md`](../validate-sweeps/2026-06-23-sweep34-iter1.md).
- `taxonomy.yml` and `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated for the breach-response per-doc version bump (the two `docs/` guides are not taxonomy-tracked, so only the breach-response row changed in the generated artefacts).

### Verification
- Mechanical baseline clean before and after (`tools/run_all_audits.sh`: all 47 gates passed). Clone was unshallowed before the history-aware gates 31/40.
- Subagent A's findings were apply-time-verified by orchestrator re-read: the Day-1 floor's six items were enumerated against the Tier 1 table (4 present, 2 absent, confirmed by `grep -c` returning 0 for the two missing policies in `docs/adopter-guide.md`); the breach-response table's "DPO, with Legal" rows confirmed against the §4.2 lead-in. A's WebSearch "210,000" over-correction was refuted against the OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet (corpus's 220,000 is correct, not routed).
- Empty-delta primary termination at iteration 1 (the three findings were self-contained prose corrections with no further parallel surfaces).

Updated to "MAM and MDM controls". This was the sole in-window finding from PR #291's `/validate-pr` cross-reference check (the index register is hand-maintained, not generated, so the descriptor was hand-authored and made stale by #291).

## 2026-06-23, Library Version 2026.06.269, PR #291

**PR-F of the XS/S batch: a six-item security and crypto bundle (FR-153, BYOD MDM/MAM, FR-90, FR-84, FR-85, FR-86), built as one PR per maintainer direction.** A research worker surfaced each item's defect, fix shape, and directional choices, and verified the OWASP PBKDF2 values against the canonical OWASP GitHub source (HTML egress was 403-blocked); the orchestrator re-read every target file at apply-time. Four directional choices were maintainer-confirmed: FR-153 PBKDF2 target = SHA-256 at 600,000 (SHA-512 at 220,000 noted, Argon2id preferred); BYOD kept in PR-F as a structural rewrite; FR-90 and FR-84 done in-document (not as new standalone artefacts); FR-85 leads distributed across DPO / CISO / Legal.

### Changed
- [`security/policy-encryption-and-key-management.md`](../../security/policy-encryption-and-key-management.md) (`1.3.3` → `1.3.4`): **FR-153**. The section-3 "Password-based encryption" row read "PBKDF2 or Argon2id with minimum 310,000 iterations" with no HMAC hash named, leaving the OWASP-correct count ambiguous (the target is hash-dependent). Now reads: Argon2id (preferred) at minimum m=19456 (19 MiB), t=2, p=1; or PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 at minimum 600,000 iterations (PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 at minimum 220,000), per the OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet. OWASP values verified against the canonical OWASP CheatSheetSeries GitHub source at apply-time.
- [`security/policy-byod.md`](../../security/policy-byod.md) (`1.0.1` → `1.1.0`): **BYOD (maintainer-directed)**. Reframed from a single MAM-without-MDM posture to present two adopter-selectable deployment models. A new "BYOD deployment models" section defines MAM (container model) and MDM (full-device model, requiring recorded enrolment consent) and states the adopter chooses one or both. The Purpose, governance table, Approved access model intro, "What the organisation does not do", incident/loss wipe, offboarding, policy-violations, and NIST SP 800-124r2 framework-alignment relevance are all rescoped from MAM-only to model-conditional. The data-classification ceiling (no Confidential on personal devices without CISO approval) is left as the conservative default for an adopter to relax. The MAM-only assertions were woven through ~6 sections, so this was a full-document re-read, not a localized insert.
- [`dev-security/standard-developer-security-requirements.md`](../../dev-security/standard-developer-security-requirements.md) (`1.0.2` → `1.1.0`): **FR-90**. Section 5 (input validation and output encoding) gains two browser-hardening requirements: a Content-Security-Policy that disallows inline script by default and adopts Trusted Types where supported, and the transport/content-type hardening headers (HSTS with `preload`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `frame-ancestors`/`X-Frame-Options`). Added as bullets within section 5 (output-hardening theme) rather than a new top-level section, to avoid renumbering sections 6 to 16 and their cross-references. The guidance previously existed only in the supplementary addyosmani overlay, not the citable primary corpus.
- [`operations/procedure-patch-management.md`](../../operations/procedure-patch-management.md) (`1.0.1` → `1.0.2`): **FR-84**. Section 2.3's regression-testing checklist, previously four inline prose bullets, is reformatted into a numbered tick-through table (Check / Pass-Fail / Notes) that a tester records results against and retains with the change record. Done in-document (the maintainer chose this over spawning a new standalone checklist artefact).
- [`privacy/procedure-data-protection-and-privacy-breach-response.md`](../../privacy/procedure-data-protection-and-privacy-breach-response.md) (`1.4.11` → `1.4.12`): **FR-85**. The six section-4.2 24-hour-initial-assessment determinations, previously all owned "jointly" by DPO and CISO, now carry a per-question lead in a table: DPO leads data-involvement, scope, risk-to-individuals; CISO leads the access/exfiltration determination; DPO-with-Legal leads jurisdiction and notification-likelihood. The "jointly conducted" umbrella is retained.
- [`resilience/template-recovery-runbook.md`](../../resilience/template-recovery-runbook.md) (`1.0.2` → `1.0.3`): **FR-86**. Section 7 (Communications), previously a self-contained cadence table, now cross-references [`resilience/plan-crisis-communication.md`](../../resilience/plan-crisis-communication.md) (the authoritative crisis-communications plan, confirmed to exist at apply-time) for message content, spokesperson designation, and the approval chain, so the runbook and the plan do not drift. (This was the Pass-1 ⚠ item whose runbook the prior reviewer "could not locate"; it is `template-recovery-runbook.md` section 7.)

### Verification
- Research-assistant discipline: every worker claim re-verified at apply-time. The FR-153 OWASP values were re-confirmed; the FR-86 cross-reference target (`plan-crisis-communication.md`) was confirmed to exist (a dedicated crisis-communications plan, the right target over the BCM plan, which the orchestrator grep showed has no crisis-comms section); the BYOD cross-reference ripple was checked (`standard-remote-working-security.md:177` says "mobile device management where technically feasible", which the MDM/MAM-choice rewrite resolves rather than contradicts).
- Compute-don't-ask: the FR-86 target and the BYOD ripple were computed rather than asked, narrowing the maintainer's decisions to four genuine judgement calls (FR-153 hash, BYOD packaging, FR-90/84 in-doc-vs-new-artefact, FR-85 owner mapping).
- Six items rotated TODO → DONE in the same diff. Per-doc Version and Date bumped in the same commit for all six touched documents.
- `tools/run_all_audits.sh` and `tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh` results recorded at commit time; no generated-artefact regeneration was required (the six touched documents are policies/procedures/standards/templates whose taxonomy/scorecard entries update on Version, so `taxonomy.yml` + `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` were regenerated).

### Discipline observations
- FR-153's load-bearing defect was not the stale iteration count but the unspecified HMAC hash: the OWASP-correct minimum depends on the hash (SHA-256=600,000; SHA-512=220,000), so "update 310,000 to 600,000" would have been wrong if the policy intended SHA-512. Surfacing the hash ambiguity to the maintainer before editing prevented shipping a number that did not match the (now-named) hash.

## 2026-06-23, Library Version 2026.06.268, PR #290

**PR-E of the XS/S batch: a ten-item adopter and docs-UX bundle (FR-64/65/66/78/152/69/68/156/157/158), built as one PR per maintainer direction with each directional choice confirmed (recommended option) before authoring.** A research worker surfaced each item's defect, fix shape, and directional choices; the orchestrator re-read every target file at apply-time and verified every exemplar path and tool-file link before authoring. Four directional choices were confirmed by the maintainer: FR-65 adopter gate posture = relax-and-document; FR-69 = minimal nesting cross-refs now (the High-severity FR-140 nesting model has not shipped); FR-64 = name jurisdiction + sector exemplar annexes; FR-152 = startup-roadmap as a separate "add later" path.

### Changed
- [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../../CONTRIBUTING.md) (`1.2.0` → `1.2.1`): **FR-64**. "What contributions are welcome" now states that the most common net-new artefact is a sector/jurisdiction overlay and points the contributor at exemplars to mirror: a privacy jurisdiction annex (e.g. [`privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-european-union.md`](../../privacy/jurisdictions/annex-privacy-european-union.md)) for a new jurisdiction, and a compliance sector annex ([`compliance/telecommunications/annex-telecommunications-sector-requirements.md`](../../compliance/telecommunications/annex-telecommunications-sector-requirements.md) or [`compliance/public-sector/annex-fedramp-requirements.md`](../../compliance/public-sector/annex-fedramp-requirements.md)) for a new sector. All three exemplar paths verified to exist at apply-time.
- [`docs/adopter-guide.md`](../../docs/adopter-guide.md) (`1.2.0` → `1.3.0`): **FR-65** "Tracking upstream updates" expanded with three customised-fork cases (merge conflicts in adopter-edited artefacts; a new upstream gate the fork fails, resolved by the relax-and-document posture the maintainer chose; version-monotonicity conflicts). **FR-66** new "Running the audit toolchain on your fork" subsection naming [`tools/lint-pii-in-content.py`](../../tools/lint-pii-in-content.py) and [`tools/lint-secrets-in-content.py`](../../tools/lint-secrets-in-content.py) as the gates that assume a neutral corpus, with the `DEFAULT_EXEMPT_DIRS` mechanism in [`tools/lint_common.py`](../../tools/lint_common.py) as the remedy (all three tool paths verified). **FR-156** new "Enforcement and disciplinary clauses" row in the "What to change" table (breadth confirmed corpus-wide: risk policy section 9 plus the privacy jurisdiction annexes). **FR-158** the severity/notification-window row's cell now adds shortest-binding-window guidance for multi-regulator overlap (no fuller corpus treatment existed to link to; verified by grep, so an inline note rather than a cross-reference). **FR-69** the Tier 1 (15-document) description now cross-references the six-artefact Day-1 floor and the larger sector-conditional sets, stating the sizes nest.
- [`governance/framework-document-architecture-and-interrelationship.md`](../../governance/framework-document-architecture-and-interrelationship.md) (`1.1.0` → `1.1.1`): **FR-78**. The Purpose sentence's "disconnected documents performing audit theatre with nicer fonts" maintainer aside is neutralized to "a collection of disconnected documents", restoring the organisation-neutral register expected of a Framework. (Also cleared a stale `Date` of 2026-05-28.)
- [`docs/template-quickstart.md`](../../docs/template-quickstart.md) (`3.0.1` → `3.0.2`): **FR-152** "Next steps" reordered to the portal's canonical workflow order (decision tree, then implementation roadmap, then adopter guide), with a framing sentence and the startup roadmap set apart as the "add later" composition path (portal.md is generated; not hand-edited). **FR-157** "DPO" expanded to "DPO (Data Protection Officer)" on the Day-1 path. **FR-69** the six-artefact floor now noted as the Day-1 floor that nests inside the Tier 1 and sector-conditional sets.
- [`docs/decision-tree.md`](../../docs/decision-tree.md) (`1.0.4` → `1.0.5`): **FR-69**. The "about 25 documents" EU-fintech answer is now labelled sector-conditional and cross-referenced as building on (not replacing) the six-artefact floor and the 15-document Tier 1 set.
- [`docs/template-startup-roadmap.md`](../../docs/template-startup-roadmap.md) (`2.2.0` → `2.2.1`): **FR-68**. Example 2's "Core baseline (mandatory), minus the A1 elements" wording is corrected: A1 (in-house software development) is a stacking module, not a baseline element, so it is the module that does not apply, not a subtraction from the mandatory baseline. This resolves the literal incoherence with the example's own result line, which retains all six baseline artefacts.

### Verification
- Research-assistant discipline: every worker claim re-verified at apply-time. The three FR-64 exemplar annex paths and the three FR-66 tool-file paths were confirmed to exist before any link was written (no decorative/unverified links). The FR-158 "no existing multi-regulator treatment to link to" conclusion was confirmed by corpus grep before choosing an inline note.
- Compute-don't-ask: the portal-generation boundary (FR-152), enforcement-clause breadth (FR-156), multi-regulator-treatment existence (FR-158), and FR-140 shipped-status (FR-69) were all computed by the orchestrator rather than asked, narrowing the maintainer's decisions to four genuine judgement calls.
- Ten items rotated TODO → DONE in the same diff. Per-doc Version and Date bumped in the same commit for all six touched documents.
- `tools/run_all_audits.sh` and `tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh` results recorded at commit time; `taxonomy.yml` + `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated for the six per-doc bumps.

### Discipline observations
- The TODO pointers for FR-68 and FR-69 had drifted: FR-68's live defect is in `template-startup-roadmap.md`, not the quickstart the r1 pointer named (the quickstart was rewritten to 3.0.1), and its real shape is a wording incoherence rather than the "mandatory-except-when-not" the TODO title implied; FR-69 overlaps the unshipped High-severity FR-140, so it was scoped to minimal nesting cross-refs pending FR-140. Both drifts were caught at apply-time and surfaced to the maintainer.

## 2026-06-23, Library Version 2026.06.267, PR #289

**Sweep 33 (`/validate`) close-out: clarify a CIS-benchmark column label in the cloud-security baseline.** The `/resume` compensating-control corpus-wide `/validate` for the new session, the standing control for the session-closing handoff PR #288, which skipped its trailing `/validate-pr` per CLAUDE.md PR-workflow step 5a's handoff-PR exception. The #287 `/validate-pr` + `/retro` rows were already carried by #288, so this close-out carries only the Sweep 33 artefacts and the one in-window finding-fix the sweep produced.

### Changed
- [`operations/standard-cloud-security-configuration-baseline.md`](../../operations/standard-cloud-security-configuration-baseline.md) (`1.4.5` → `1.4.6`): §8's per-section ISO/CIS/NIST mapping table (added in PR #287) used the single column header "CIS Cloud Foundations area", but the §5 row maps the cloud-productivity baseline to CIS areas ("Email and collaboration security; storage; endpoint protection") that the same section's framework table attributes to the separately-named **CIS Cloud Productivity Foundations Benchmark v3.0**, not the **CIS Cloud Foundations Benchmark v2.0**. The column header is generalized to "CIS benchmark area" (the table rows span both named benchmarks; the generic header loses no information) and the preceding prose is changed from "CIS Cloud Foundations Benchmark recommendation numbers are version-specific" to name which benchmark governs each section (Cloud Foundations for §4/§6, Cloud Productivity Foundations for §5). No control identifiers changed; the ISO A.8.9/A.8.23/A.8.24 and NIST CM-2/CM-6 mappings were verified accurate during the sweep and left intact.

### Verification
- **Sweep 33 dispatch**: full three-subagent fan-out (A recent-PR deep review, B corpus-wide stale-reference, C audit-programme integrity); no abbreviation. Mechanical baseline `tools/run_all_audits.sh` = **47/47** at sweep start; pre-flight scanner 378 files, 9 candidates all confirmed false positives (pairing/comparative/historical prose).
- **Subagent A**: 1 in-window note (the finding fixed here) + 8 verified-clean cross-citations (CAPA §7.5 ↔ policy §4.3 "90 days"; internal-audit ↔ audit-planning 15-business-day extension; incident-command checklists grounded line-by-line; risk-vocabulary harmonization clean; etc.). A's out-of-scope "doubled CC BY-SA 4.0" observation **refuted** at apply-time (orchestrator corpus-wide grep `CC BY-SA 4.0 CC BY-SA 4.0` = zero hits); not routed.
- **Subagent B**: 0 findings; live counts 47/10/15/8 verified against spec §6, pack README tree, and `ls .claude/commands/`; the Sweep 32 "monitoring" lead validated against the live file and confirmed already-fixed in #286.
- **Subagent C**: 0 findings; live gate count 47 established by counting `^run_gate` in the runner, §6 inventory matches in name+order; #286's spec edit clean (the "§10" in its commit subject is a benign git-history mislabel, it edited §9 step 4; corpus content correct).
- **Apply-time re-read** of the one finding location confirmed the framework-table-vs-mapping-table imprecision before the fix; `tools/run_all_audits.sh` re-run **47/47** on the committed state; `tools/run-pr-time-checks.sh` (D1/D2/D3 + gate 45) pass; `taxonomy.yml` + `docs/maturity-scorecard.md` regenerated for the per-doc bump.

### Discipline observations
- Findings-producing `/validate` gets its own close-out PR (matching #277/#286), keeping the queued PR-E (adopter/docs UX bundle) clean per the "always split when in doubt" discipline. The single note is in-window (introduced #287), so it received a concrete fix rather than backlog routing.
